entries.title: Skills vs MCP vs Hooks: Choosing the Right Harness Component
entries.description: Skills vs MCP vs hooks compared — what each harness component does, when to use which, and how they compose. A decision guide for agent builders: knowledge goes in skills, external access goes through MCP, guarantees go in hooks.

entries.title: senior-fable
entries.description: Tech-lead orchestration mode: the top-tier model keeps architecture and decisions, cheap subagents do the routine and the digging. Save tokens without losing quality.

entries.title: plan-review
entries.description: Review and iterate on Claude's plans using inline browser comments instead of linear chat.

entries.title: microsoft-365-mcp
entries.description: Multi-account, token-efficient MCP for Outlook mail + Calendar + OneDrive via Microsoft Graph. 33 tools, Keychain-backed tokens.

entries.title: mode-marketing-ua

entries.title: pam-core

entries.title: instantsearch
entries.description: Build production-quality search experiences (autocomplete, search results pages, faceted search) using InstantSearch with React, Vue, or vanilla JS. Covers discovery, implementation, styling, and review with expert guidance on widget selection, styling approaches, mobile overlays, and common anti-patterns. Do NOT use for backend index operations — use algolia-cli. Do NOT use for analytics or recommendations — use algolia-mcp. Do NOT use for AI/agent/conversational search — use algobot-cli.

entries.title: ship

entries.title: harness-humanizer
entries.description: Removes AI-slop from prose (empty hedging, listicle stems, smooth transitions, generic filler) and rewrites toward a real point of view — without trading AI-slop for louder slop. Self-scores against an embedded rubric, flags hollow spans instead of fabricating, and reports rather than overwrites. Portable to every AI tool via generated adapters; ships a zero-dependency CLI flagger.

entries.title: repo-wiki

entries.title: agentos
entries.description: Companion layer for AI coding agents: review queue delivered when the agent finishes, locked project decisions injected into every prompt. Requires the agentos binary on PATH (cargo install --path crates/agentos-cli, or a release binary).

entries.title: teachme
entries.description: Teach Me — turn any topic into a searchable, themed handbook or study material (reference · practice · narrative chapters · quiz/Q&A) written in the user's language from official sources, quality-verified by a multi-agent harness, and published to GitHub Pages. Invoke with /teachme. Requires Claude Code, git, gh (GitHub CLI); GitHub Pro assumed for private repo + public Pages.

Caliper — precision for your AI spend: a local dashboard showing exactly where your Claude Code money goes (sessions, workflows, subagents, machine-wide analytics; cache-aware cost reconstruction), plus /optimize-spend to turn your usage into cost discipline. 100% local, no telemetry.

entries.title: explainmyrepo
entries.description: Turn any GitHub repo into a beautiful, art-directed explainer site that takes a stranger from "I've never seen this" to "oh, I get why this matters — and I'm ready to use it." One Claude Code skill (the brain) reads the repo, builds a real RVF vector knowledge base, conceives a bespoke visual metaphor, authors the comprehension arc, generates raster + SVG visuals, assembles a responsive page on a shared design system, grades it on real pixels against a dual quality gate (>=95 on mobile and desktop), deploys it to its own URL, and ships a downloadable AI knowledge pack so the reader's AI gets it too. Pure, independently-testable tools do the mechanics; one BuildContext carries the work.

entries.title: larch
entries.description: Multi-agent workflow automation for Claude Code: issue-anchored `/design` (one direct-drafting flow; plan-review panel), `/bug` (consumer issue-filing helper), `/deps` issue dependency audits, and `/implement` (positional `<issue-N>`, fixed 7200s Step 2 timeout, optional `--merge`, `--forked`, `--draft`, `--no-admin-fallback`, `--no-logs-commit`, `--coder`, `--run-id`). `/implement` Preflight reads the `larch:plan` block from the issue body; `/implement` Step 5 code review always uses the unified strict panel internally (public `--panel` argv removed).

entries.title: superpipelines
entries.description: Design, generate, and run multi-agent AI pipelines — scope-aware, multi-pipeline, spec-driven, write/review-isolated, with step-level create/update/delete and integrated audit

entries.title: dolphin

entries.title: e2e-skills
entries.description: AI agent testing toolkit to generate, review, and debug Playwright and Cypress E2E tests. Four agent skills: generate tests from scratch with live browser exploration, explicit user approval, and first-run scaffolding of project testing conventions (AGENTS.md E2E section + seed spec) for consistent AI generation across Claude Code, Codex, and Playwright Agents; review existing specs against 24 anti-patterns grouped by P0/P1/P2 severity — P0 must-fix (name-assertion mismatch, missing Then, error swallowing, Cypress uncaught:exception suppression, always-passing assertions, bypass patterns, focused test leak, missing assertions, missing auth setup, missing await on expect, missing await on action), P1 should-fix (raw DOM queries, hard-coded sleeps, flaky test patterns, inconsistent POM usage, hardcoded credentials, direct page action API, expect.soft overuse, module-level mutable state in test utilities, unmocked real-backend writes, optimistic UI without call proof), P2 nice-to-fix (YAGNI + zombie specs, manually-captured session-file dependency, fixture ignores render guards); debug failed Playwright tests from playwright-report/; debug failed Cypress tests from cypress/reports/.

entries.title: zeromind
entries.description: ZeroMind MCP plugin: ZeroMind content discovery (zeromind.search/inspect/install/engage — find, reuse, review, and vote on published worlds + assets) plus world list/create/launch/connect and engine tools (execute, guides, capture, VFS, bash) routed to the WASM engine in your browser. Bundled skills teach Claude to check ZeroMind first and drive Zero worlds. REQUIRES Node.js 18+ — if you see 'status failed', install Node from https://nodejs.org and restart Claude Code.

entries.title: cowork-to-code-bridge
entries.description: Connect Claude Cowork to Claude Code on your own computer (macOS or Linux). Cowork hands a task to a local Claude Code agent (and can run approved scripts too); the bridge is idempotent, streams progress, and is token-protected. Install with one command; it sets up a global Cowork skill so every session connects automatically — no /plugin, no setup inside Cowork.

entries.title: sprint
entries.description: Autonomous TODO workflow: refine an idea into one structured TODO item, then a git-hook chain plans → implements (subagent team + deterministic gate, isolated worktree, never auto-merged) → reviews it. Language-agnostic, config-driven, bootstrapped into any repo.

entries.title: fablewise
entries.description: Orchestration multi-modèles pilotée par le coût : Fable conçoit, challenge et arbitre ; Sonnet/Haiku exécutent et vérifient. Quatre commandes : /plan (concevoir un plan validé), /plan-run (l'exécuter tâche par tâche avec vérification, escalade et suivi de progression), /plan-rework (fusionner d'anciens plans, rechallenger leurs demandes, refondre l'ensemble), /plan-prompt (un prompt optimal + le modèle recommandé pour une tâche unique).

entries.description: Hybrid pipeline for spec-driven development with Japanese design documents + Figma. Wraps GitHub Spec Kit with a design-intake agent and a code-reviewer agent.

entries.description: Auto-dub videos into other languages with lip-sync via the Perso AI Dubbing API. Handles single files, folders, URLs, oversized/long media (auto split & merge), multi-language output, and resume.

entries.title: ai-agent-skills
entries.description: Disciplined agent workflows: prompt sharpening, structured consultation (kabeuchi), goal-driven execution, cost-tiered orchestration, and role-based dev orchestration.

entries.title: killhouse

entries.description: ESG benchmarking for lenders, CMBS underwriters, and insurers — peer EUI/ENERGY STAR percentile screening, lender ESG cards, and green financing eligibility across loan books and collateral pools.

entries.title: ripple
entries.description: Agent video editing: transcription-driven cuts, frame-sheet eyes, HDR-safe finishing, deterministic QA, and a persistent taste memory (VIDEO.md). Routes motion graphics to HyperFrames or Remotion.

entries.title: growth-engine
entries.description: A personal growth strategist for builders and creators: opportunity radar, niche research wikis, brand positioning, accountability reviews, and a self-improving playbook — all with persistent memory across sessions.

entries.title: centauri
entries.description: A deep harness for LLM-powered CLIs. Once loaded in a session, every substantive task runs through an orchestrator that spawns parallel subagents, adversarially verifies their work, keeps rolling summaries, and persists what it learns into a file-based semantic layer (.centauri/) that reloads every session.
